prompts.label: Hello World compile and run
prompts.text:
I'm learning C (C11/C17). Create hello.c printing Hello, World.

- Compile with gcc hello.c -o hello and run
- Explain #include <stdio.h> and int main(void)
- Warnings: gcc -Wall -Wextra -pedantic

No C++ features.

prompts.label: Types printf and scanf safely
prompts.text:
Teach int, double, char, and printf format specifiers (%d, %f, %c).

- Read an integer with scanf and check return value
- Warn about buffer overflows with %s without width
- Use fixed buffer char name[32] with %31s

Small runnable program.

prompts.label: Pointers and addresses
prompts.text:
Explain pointers for beginners with a swap function using pointers.

- Diagram in comments: variable, address, pointer
- NULL pointer check habit
- One common bug: returning pointer to local stack variable — why it's wrong

Two short examples.

prompts.label: Arrays and C strings
prompts.text:
Work with char buffers and string.h.

- Initialize array, iterate with index and with pointer arithmetic (brief)
- strlen, strncpy instead of strcpy — explain why
- Null terminator '\0' importance

Include one safer string copy example.

prompts.label: Headers and multiple .c files
prompts.text:
Split a tiny project into math.h, math.c, and main.c.

- Include guards #ifndef MATH_H
- Declarations in .h, definitions in .c
- Compile: gcc -c math.c && gcc main.c math.o -o app

Explain translation units in one paragraph.

prompts.label: malloc calloc free
prompts.text:
Build a dynamic array of integers that grows.

- malloc, realloc, free — always free what you malloc
- Check for NULL after allocation
- Contrast stack array vs heap array when to use each

Minimal working code with error handling.

prompts.label: struct and typedef
prompts.text:
Model a 2D Point with struct and functions distance(Point a, Point b).

- typedef struct { ... } Point;
- Pass struct by value vs pointer — when pointer saves copies
- Initialize with designated initializers C99 style

No OOP keywords.

prompts.label: File I/O fopen fread fwrite
prompts.text:
Read a text file line by line in C.

- fopen with mode "r", fclose in all paths
- fgets loop, handle EOF
- Check fopen != NULL and perror on failure

Count lines in sample.txt example.

prompts.label: Command line argc argv
prompts.text:
Parse command-line arguments without getopt first, then mention getopt.

- Program copy.c: copy source dest paths from argv
- Print usage if argc < 3
- Simple -h help flag

Show example shell commands.

prompts.label: Makefile for small project
prompts.text:
Write a Makefile for main.c + utils.c project.

- Variables CC, CFLAGS (-Wall -g), TARGET, OBJS
- Pattern rules .c.o and link step
- make, make clean phony target

Explain why Make still matters for embedded/Linux C.

prompts.label: enum and preprocessor macros
prompts.text:
Use enum for Status codes and #define for constants.

- Prefer enum over magic numbers
- Macro pitfalls: parenthesize arguments, no semicolon after macro
- const int vs #define tradeoff in modern C

Short status-to-string helper using switch.

prompts.label: const static extern linkage
prompts.text:
Explain static functions hiding linkage in a .c file.

- const parameters and const pointers (const char * vs char * const)
- extern declaration across files
- Why global mutable state is discouraged

Tiny two-file demo.

prompts.label: Function pointers callback
prompts.text:
Introduce function pointers with a sort comparator or foreach callback.

- Typedef for int (*CompareFn)(const void*, const void*)
- Pass callback to apply_to_array function
- Link to qsort as real-world example

Keep readable for first-time learners.

prompts.label: errno and perror
prompts.text:
Handle errors from library calls using errno.

- #include <errno.h>, perror("fopen")
- Check return values systematically
- Do not mix errno across threads without mention (teaser)

Example failing to open missing file.

prompts.label: Binary fread fwrite structs
prompts.text:
Write and read a struct to a binary file.

- fwrite(&record, sizeof(record), 1, fp)
- Warn about struct padding and portability — not for cross-platform without care
- Prefer portable serialization for real projects

Educational example with one Record struct.

prompts.label: GDB debug basics
prompts.text:
Teach debugging C with gdb on a program with an off-by-one bug.

- Compile with -g
- gdb ./app, break main, run, next, print variable
- backtrace when segfault happens

List 5 essential gdb commands for beginners.

prompts.label: Valgrind leak check intro
prompts.text:
Show a deliberate memory leak and fix it using Valgrind mindset.

- valgrind --leak-check=full ./app
- Read "definitely lost" output
- Fix missing free in error path

Mention AddressSanitizer as modern alternative one line.

prompts.label: stdint.h fixed-width types
prompts.text:
Explain stdint.h types for portable integers.

- uint8_t, int32_t, SIZE_MAX from stdint.h / stddef.h
- When char might be signed or unsigned
- Use in a simple byte buffer checksum function

Embedded-friendly tone.

prompts.label: Bitwise operators flags
prompts.text:
Implement feature flags with bitwise OR, AND, NOT.

- #define FLAG_A (1u << 0)
- Functions has_flag, set_flag, clear_flag
- Print flags in hex for debugging

Relate to embedded register bits briefly.

prompts.label: When to choose C
prompts.text:
Honest guide: when learning C is worth it vs C++/Rust/Python.

- Embedded, OS kernels, existing C codebases, FFI boundary
- Manual memory management costs
- 5 bullet learning path after basics

No code — decision guide.
